title: An identical payload within 24h returns the cached job for free (from_cache) — don't re-submit in a loop
summary: The job-submit layer hashes the payload and, if the same payload was submitted within the last 24 hours, returns the prior job instead of running (and billing) a new one — the submit response carries from_cache: true. For paid people calls this is a real cost lever: re-reading a profile/search/company you already ran today costs nothing and returns instantly. Conversely, re-submitting the SAME payload expecting 'fresher' data just returns the cache — to force a genuinely new scrape you must change a field (e.g. bump max_employees, change profile_mode, tweak the query). Cost per fresh run: profile ~$0.003, search ~$0.01, company $4/$8/$12 per 1K employees.
references: app/api/v1/jobs/common.py:514 — 'Automatic deduplication: Returns cached job if submitted within 24 hours'
app/api/v1/jobs/common.py:584-606 — job hash computed from payload; cached hit returns from_cache=True
